_description: Olympus pipeline example: known-good golden artifact demonstrating all pipeline stages (Ingest → Canonicalize → Hash → Commit → Prove → Verify). Hash values are deterministic and must not change without a protocol version bump.
_note: Replicate (Guardian multi-node replication) is a Phase 1+ feature and is not represented here.

stage_2_canonicalize.description: Deterministic JSON canonicalization (RFC 8785 / JCS): keys sorted, compact separators, ensure_ascii=True
stage_2_canonicalize.canonical_json: {"agency":"NARA","body":"This record was filed on 2024-01-15.","classification":"UNCLASSIFIED","id":"DOC-2024-00001","title":"Example Government Record"}

stage_3_hash.description: Domain-separated BLAKE3 hash of canonical JSON bytes (UTF-8)

stage_4_commit.description: Merkle tree commitment over document sections. Leaf hashes use domain prefix OLY:LEAF:V1.

stage_5_prove.description: Merkle inclusion proof for leaf index 0 (section-1 text)
stage_5_prove.proof_note: Run `python -c 'from protocol.merkle import MerkleTree, verify_proof; ...'` to verify.

stage_6_replicate.description: NOT IMPLEMENTED IN v1.0 — Guardian replication is a Phase 1+ feature.

stage_7_verify.description: Independent verification: reconstruct canonical JSON, recompute hashes, check Merkle root and ledger chain.
verification_steps: 1. Canonicalize the document to reproduce canonical_json (stage 2)
2. Hash canonical_json bytes with BLAKE3 to reproduce document_hash_blake3 (stage 3)
3. Compute merkle_leaf_hashes using BLAKE3(OLY:LEAF:V1 | leaf_data) for each section
4. Build Merkle tree and verify merkle_root (stage 4)
5. Canonicalize the ledger entry payload JSON and hash with BLAKE3(OLY:LEDGER:V1 | payload_bytes) to reproduce entry_hash
6. Run `make vectors` to verify all golden test vectors pass
